Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration To Pltfs' Individual Claims And To Dismiss The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: G. ARTHUR MENESES, ESQ., 310.556.5637, FOR PLAINTIFF SCOTT WILLIAMS.
PAUL W. SWEENEY JR., ESQ., 310.552.5055, FOR DEFENDANTS US BANCORP INVESTMENTS, INC. & U.S. BANCORP.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP INVESTMENTS, INC. & U.S. BANCORP'S Motion To Compel Arbitration To PlAINtIFfs' Individual Claims And To Dismiss The First Amended Complaint is denied without prejudice. Defendants' Requests for Judicial Notice are granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. FINRA Rule 13204(d) prohibits the enforcement of an arbitration agreement with respect to any claim that is the subject of a putative class action until there is a determination of class certification made. Collateral estoppel does not apply here, as the first prong of the test requiring that the issues be identical is not established. (Johnson v. GlaxoSmithKline, Inc. (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1497, 1507-08.) The Burakoff class was comprised of "all individuals who are or were employed as Investment Financial Consultants in the State of California at any time during the period commencing October 13, 2001 to [May 8, 2008]." (May 25, 2011 Order re: Defendant US Bancorp's Motion for Decertification in LASC Case no. BC341430.) The putative class in this case is comprised of "all commission paid employees who worked for Defendants in California from May 9, 2008 until the date of certification." (First Amended Complaint, 15.) The issue of whether the class in this case can be certified is not identical to the issue of decertification of the Burakoff class because the two classes are comprised of different class members during different time periods. Alvarez v. May Dept. Stores Co. ((2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1223, 1237) and Johnson v. GlaxoSmithKline, Inc. ((2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1497, 1507-08), both cited by Defendants in support of their collateral estoppel argument, state that collateral estoppel applies where a party seeks to certify an identical class. Because the class here is not identical, collateral estoppel does not apply. Therefore, no determination of class certification has been made at this point in the proceedings. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: G. ARTHUR MENESES, ESQ., 310.556.5637, FOR PLAINTIFF SCOTT WILLIAMS.
PAUL W. SWEENEY JR., ESQ., 310.552.5055, FOR DEFENDANTS US BANCORP INVESTMENTS, INC. & U.S. BANCORP.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP INVESTMENTS, INC. & U.S. BANCORP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for unpaid business expenses. Plaintiff does not contest dismissal of this claim. (Pl's Opp. to Demurrer to First Amended Complaint; p. 1, line 18-21.) The demurrer is overruled as to all other causes of action. Collateral estoppel does not apply here, as the first prong of the test requiring that the issues be identical is not established. (Johnson v. GlaxoSmithKline, Inc. (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1497, 1507-08.) The Burakoff class was comprised of "all individuals who are or were employed as Investment Financial Consultants in the State of California at any time during the period commencing October 13, 2001 to [May 8, 2008]." (May 25, 2011 Order re: Defendant US Bancorp's Motion for Decertification in LASC Case no. BC341430.) The putative class in this case is comprised of "all commission paid employees who worked for Defendants in California from May 9, 2008 until the date of certification." (First Amended Complaint, 15.) The issue of whether the class in this case can be certified is not identical to the issue of decertification of the Burakoff class because the two classes are comprised of different class members during different time periods. Alvarez v. May Dept. Stores Co. ((2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1223, 1237) and Johnson v. GlaxoSmithKline, Inc. ((2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1497, 1507-08), both cited by Defendants in support of their collateral estoppel argument, state that collateral estoppel applies where a party seeks to certify an identical class. Because the class here is not identical, collateral estoppel does not apply. Additionally, Plaintiff has sufficiently pled that his claims are typical of all class members. (First Amended Complaint, 17(b).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
